title: Binder end-of-life: retire delivered binders
summary: When a binder finishes delivering, karta should archive it and retire its slug so finished work stops appearing as pending in status, on the watch page, and in the validator. This binder rebuilds the shipped v1.17.0 feature as a karta binder — a ground-truth dogfood of karta running on karta itself.
motivation: Prove karta can plan and deliver a real karta feature by reconstructing one we already have ground truth for.

included: The status engine (karta_next.py) learning that archived binders are done, not pending
The Karta Watch page (serve_status.py) keeping delivered binders visible as history
The binder validator (validate_binder.py) resolving archived predecessors and warning on reused slugs
The read-only guard hook (guard_binder_immutability.py) extended to the archive folder
The deliver-time archival step in karta-deliver (Phase 6c) and its single-item twin in karta-build
The accompanying doc updates (hooks how-to, binder reference) and the 3-way skill mirrors
excluded: The plugin version bump and the release checklist — those live in a separate release commit, not this feature
Any change to Codex agent projections under .codex/agents — this feature touches no agents

Delivering on the current tree: the feature already exists here, so a real dogfood run first resets the working tree to the 710c714 (v1.16.0) merge — the commit before d904e9f — then delivers this binder and diffs the result against d904e9f

design_facts.stack: karta plugin repo — Python 3.11+ stdlib scripts run via `uv run --script`, each carrying an embedded `--self-test` mode that prints [PASS]/[FAIL] lines and an `N/N checks passed` tally; Markdown SKILL.md skills with 3-way byte-identical mirrors (skills/ -> .agents/skills/ -> plugins/karta/skills/) regenerated by sync_codex_skills.py; JSON binder and manifest files. Ships as both a Claude Code plugin and a Codex CLI plugin.

work_items.title: Teach the status engine to read the archive
work_items.summary: The status engine treats a delivered binder in the archive as finished, so it stops listing completed work as pending.
work_items.contract: karta_next.py exposes `load_archived_binders(archive_dir=ARCHIVE_DIR) -> list[dict]`, reading `.karta/binders/archive/*.json`. The next-action / topo derivation treats an `after` edge that names an archived slug as satisfied rather than dangling, and a live binder whose slug matches an archived one emits a shadow warning. The archive directory is the single source both this engine and the watch page read.

assertions: exit 0 with all [PASS] lines and no [FAIL]
an `after` edge naming an archived slug resolves as satisfied, not as a dangling-reference warning
a live binder reusing an archived slug produces the live-shadows-archived warning
load_archived_binders() returns the archive/*.json binders and is junk-tolerant

work_items.title: Make the validator archive-aware and warn on reused slugs
work_items.summary: The binder validator resolves archived predecessors as satisfied and warns when a new binder reuses a retired slug.
work_items.contract: validate_binder.py resolves an `after` edge that names an archived (delivered) slug as satisfied rather than dangling; warns when a live binder reuses an archived slug (the delivered history is shadowed); and, when a referenced binder file is missing but an archived twin exists, reports 'already delivered' instead of raising a traceback.

assertions: exit 0 with all [PASS] lines and no [FAIL]
an archived after-edge resolves as satisfied (no dangling warning)
a live binder reusing an archived slug emits the shadow warning
a missing binder with an archived twin explains 'already delivered' rather than raising

work_items.title: Extend the read-only guard to the archive
work_items.summary: The immutability hook treats archived binders as read-only, so delivered history can never be edited.
work_items.contract: guard_binder_immutability.py treats files under `.karta/binders/archive/` as committed and read-only, blocking Write/Edit against them exactly as it does live committed binders. docs/how-to/hooks.md documents that the guard now covers the archive folder.

assertions: exit 0 with all [PASS] lines and no [FAIL]
an edit targeting a file under .karta/binders/archive/ is blocked
the existing live-committed-binder cases still block

work_items.title: Show delivered binders on the watch page
work_items.summary: The Karta Watch page keeps delivered binders visible under its Delivered phase instead of dropping them.
work_items.contract: serve_status.py imports karta_next and calls karta_next.load_archived_binders(), joining archived binders into the served state as merged 'delivered' rows. The join is junk-tolerant and live-wins on any slug collision, so the Delivered phase keeps history without ever hiding live work.

assertions: exit 0 with all [PASS] lines and no [FAIL]
archived binders appear in the served state as delivered rows
on a slug collision the live binder wins over the archived one
malformed archive entries are tolerated, not fatal

work_items.title: Archive the binder when delivery completes
work_items.summary: A completed delivery moves its binder into the archive as a commit on the integration branch, so finished work stops showing as pending.
work_items.contract: karta-deliver gains Phase 6c ('deliver:archive'): on a complete run it runs `mkdir -p .karta/binders/archive` and `git mv`es the binder to `.karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json`, committed on the integration branch so the archival travels with the user's merge. The step is resume-idempotent and an incomplete run (deferred or halted items) leaves the binder live. The archive path and the `after`-names-an-archived-slug resolution are documented in binder-reference.md. This item also relocates any already-delivered binder still sitting in `.karta/binders/` (the kaizen-frame ghost) under the new convention.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: exit 0
the karta-deliver 3-way mirror is byte-identical (sync_codex_skills --check reports no drift)
validate_plugin --self-test passes with all [PASS] lines
binder-reference.md documents the archive location and the archived-slug after-edge resolution

work_items.title: Archive from the single-item build path too
work_items.summary: The single-item build hatch archives its binder as well, in the mode where no orchestrator exists to do it.
work_items.contract: karta-build's 9c-single step owns the same archival action as karta-deliver Phase 6c (the single-item hatch has no orchestrator), using the identical archive path and `git mv` mechanic and reporting it in Phase 10. The archival wording is stated identically to karta-deliver's — the same cross-file term, quoted, not paraphrased.

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: exit 0
the karta-build 3-way mirror is byte-identical (sync_codex_skills --check reports no drift)
validate_plugin --self-test passes with all [PASS] lines
the archival step wording matches karta-deliver Phase 6c exactly

work_items.title: Require a fresh slug at plan time
work_items.summary: Planning requires a fresh slug: an archived (delivered) slug or surviving git refs make reuse unsafe, so the doctrine directs picking a new one.
work_items.contract: karta-plan's slug rule states a slug is taken if a live binder, an archived binder, or surviving `refs/karta/<slug>/` refs / `karta/<slug>/*` tags exist, and directs picking a fresh slug instead. It cites validate_binder.py's shadow warning as the enforcement point, quoting that warning's wording rather than paraphrasing it (house-pack term-identity).

oracle.command: uv run scripts/sync_codex_skills.py --check && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: exit 0
the karta-plan 3-way mirror is byte-identical (sync_codex_skills --check reports no drift)
validate_plugin --self-test passes with all [PASS] lines
the doctrine's shadow-warning wording matches validate_binder.py's emitted message
